events.label: reasoning
events.text: We need produce JSON answer no markdown. Need assess scenario: commercial masonry flashing & weeps, observed field condition flashing not lapped/sealed, rough-complete. Need decide pass/fail based on code/spec/manufacturer. TMS 402 and BIA tech notes require flashing to be lapped and sealed to prevent water intrusion. Flashing not lapped/sealed is non-compliant, installed-defective. Decision fail. Risk? water intrusion in masonry cavity, could be high commercial, especially if unsealed laps. Work_stage rough-complete. percent_complete maybe 80. confidence 1.0. Findings: flashing not lapped/sealed. Actions: remove/rework/install lapped sealed flashing, test, inspect. References: TMS 402, BIA Tech Note 7? Actually BIA has tech notes on flashing. Provide. Need include all fields. Ensure valid JSON.

  "findings": ["Flashing is installed but laps are not overlapped and sealed at joints, terminations, and penetrations", "Unsealed flashing laps will allow water to bypass the drainage plane and enter the wall assembly"],
  "actions": ["Remove or pull back flashing as needed to properly lap and seal all joints, end dams, corners, and penetrations", "Use manufacturer-approved compatible sealant/adhesive or heat-weld seams per approved detail", "Verify slope to weeps and continuity to drainage plane before closing out wall"],
  "rationale": "TMS 402 and BIA flashing details require through-wall flashing to be continuous, lapped, and sealed so the cavity drains outward. A flashing system that is not lapped/sealed cannot perform its intended water-management function, making the installation defective and non-compliant at the rough-complete stage.",
  "references": ["TMS 402 - Building Code Requirements for Masonry Structures", "BIA Technical Notes on Brick Construction - Flashing details and recommendations"]
}
